pairs.question: When was Christy Heffernan born?
pairs.answer: Christy Heffernan was born on 26 December 1957 in Glenmore, County Kilkenny, Ireland.

pairs.question: What position did Christy Heffernan play in hurling?
pairs.answer: Christy Heffernan played as a full-forward for the Kilkenny senior hurling team throughout his career.

pairs.question: How many All-Ireland medals did Christy Heffernan win during his inter-county hurling career?
pairs.answer: Christy Heffernan won four All-Ireland medals with the Kilkenny senior hurling team.

pairs.question: What notable achievements did Christy Heffernan have at club level with Glenmore?
pairs.answer: At club level, Christy Heffernan won one All-Ireland medal, two Leinster medals, and five championship medals with Glenmore.

pairs.question: In addition to hurling, what other sport did Christy Heffernan play competitively, and what was his achievement?
pairs.answer: Christy Heffernan also played senior football for Glenmore and captained the team to victory in the 1989 county final, earning man of the match honors by scoring 5 points.

pairs.question: How many championship appearances did Christy Heffernan make for Kilkenny during his inter-county hurling career?
pairs.answer: Christy Heffernan made 32 championship appearances for the Kilkenny senior hurling team.

pairs.question: Why is Christy Heffernan considered one of Kilkenny's greatest hurling players?
pairs.answer: Christy Heffernan is widely regarded as one of Kilkenny's all-time greatest hurling players due to his four All-Ireland medals, seven Leinster medals, four National Hurling League medals, an All-Star award, and being selected at full-forward on the Kilkenny Hurling Team of the Century.
